exercises.title: Trace circuit breaker state transitions
exercises.scenario: Given failure counts, determine circuit state at each point.
exercises.instructions:
```
Config: slidingWindowSize=10, minimumNumberOfCalls=5, failureRateThreshold=50%
waitDurationInOpenState=30s, permittedNumberOfCallsInHalfOpenState=3

Calls (F=failure, S=success):
  Calls 1-4:  F, F, F, S
  Call 5:     F
  Call 6:     S
  Calls 7-10: F, F, F, F
  Call 11:    attempt
  (30 seconds pass)
  Calls 12-14: S, S, S  (half-open test)
  Call 15:    attempt
```

After each milestone, what is the circuit state?
exercises.expected_solution:
After calls 1-4: CLOSED (only 4 calls < minimumNumberOfCalls=5, no state change possible)

After call 5: Check: 5 calls = minimumNumberOfCalls met.
  Failures in window: F,F,F,S,F = 4/5 = 80% ≥ 50% → CIRCUIT OPENS!

After call 6 (attempt while OPEN): Fallback called (0ms), not counted in window.

After calls 7-10: Circuit is OPEN, all 4 calls → fallback. Not counted.

Call 11: Circuit OPEN. → fallback.

(30s elapse: waitDurationInOpenState expires)
Circuit transitions to HALF-OPEN.

Calls 12-14 (3 test calls in half-open): S, S, S → 0% failure rate.
All 3 permitted calls succeed → circuit transitions to CLOSED.

Call 15: Circuit CLOSED. Real service called normally.

test_cases.expected:
After calls 1-4: CLOSED (only 4 calls < minimumNumberOfCalls=5, no state change possible)

After call 5: Check: 5 calls = minimumNumberOfCalls met.
  Failures in window: F,F,F,S,F = 4/5 = 80% ≥ 50% → CIRCUIT OPENS!

After call 6 (attempt while OPEN): Fallback called (0ms), not counted in window.

After calls 7-10: Circuit is OPEN, all 4 calls → fallback. Not counted.

Call 11: Circuit OPEN. → fallback.

(30s elapse: waitDurationInOpenState expires)
Circuit transitions to HALF-OPEN.

Calls 12-14 (3 test calls in half-open): S, S, S → 0% failure rate.
All 3 permitted calls succeed → circuit transitions to CLOSED.

Call 15: Circuit CLOSED. Real service called normally.

hints: minimumNumberOfCalls: no state change until this many calls recorded
OPEN state: all calls → fallback, NOT recorded in sliding window
HALF-OPEN: only permittedNumberOfCalls pass; all must succeed to CLOSE

exercises.title: Complete the @CircuitBreaker annotation and fallback
exercises.scenario: Standard circuit breaker with fallback returning empty list.

blanks.hint: Attribute pointing to the fallback method name
Java 9+ immutable empty list factory method

hints: @CircuitBreaker: Resilience4j annotation, not Spring's @CircuitBreaker
fallbackMethod attribute: string with the exact method name
Exception last param: Resilience4j passes the exception that caused the fallback

exercises.title: Write application.yml circuit breaker configuration
exercises.scenario: Configure two circuit breakers with different thresholds.
exercises.instructions:
Write application.yml for:
1. 'payment-gateway': failureRate=30%, window=20, min=10, wait=60s, halfOpen=5
2. 'notification': failureRate=60%, window=10, min=5, wait=15s, halfOpen=2
3. Both with slow call detection: calls > 3s count as slow, slow rate threshold 70%
exercises.expected_solution:
# application.yml:
resilience4j:
  circuitbreaker:
    instances:
      payment-gateway:
        slidingWindowType: COUNT_BASED
        slidingWindowSize: 20
        minimumNumberOfCalls: 10
        failureRateThreshold: 30
        waitDurationInOpenState: 60s
        permittedNumberOfCallsInHalfOpenState: 5
        slowCallDurationThreshold: 3s
        slowCallRateThreshold: 70
      notification:
        slidingWindowType: COUNT_BASED
        slidingWindowSize: 10
        minimumNumberOfCalls: 5
        failureRateThreshold: 60
        waitDurationInOpenState: 15s
        permittedNumberOfCallsInHalfOpenState: 2
        slowCallDurationThreshold: 3s
        slowCallRateThreshold: 70

# payment-gateway: strict (30% failure → open) — payments are critical
# notification: lenient (60% failure → open) — notifications are non-critical
hints: slidingWindowType: COUNT_BASED (last N calls) or TIME_BASED (last N seconds)
minimumNumberOfCalls: prevents false open on early low-traffic calls
waitDurationInOpenState format: '60s' (seconds) or '1m' (minutes)

exercises.title: Circuit breaker with cached fallback
exercises.scenario: Fallback returns the last cached value when the real service is down.
exercises.instructions:
Write ZerodhaInstrumentService:
1. fetchInstrumentData(symbol): @CircuitBreaker('instrument-feed')
2. Fallback: return last known price from a local Map cache
3. updateCache(symbol, data): updates the local cache
4. The Map acts as an L0 in-memory cache for degraded mode
5. What is the risk of returning stale cached data?
hints: ConcurrentHashMap: thread-safe for multi-threaded request handling
Update cache on success: ensures lastKnownData is always the freshest successful response
Stale flag: mark the returned data so callers know it may be outdated

exercises.title: Multiple circuit breakers in one service
exercises.scenario: A service calling multiple external APIs — each gets its own circuit breaker.
exercises.instructions:
Write BookMyShow EventService:
1. getEventDetails(eventId): @CircuitBreaker('event-db') → fallback empty optional
2. getSeatMap(eventId): @CircuitBreaker('seat-service') → fallback zone pricing
3. getVenueInfo(venueId): @CircuitBreaker('venue-db') → fallback cached venue
4. Different configs for each — seat-service is strictest (real-time critical)
5. Show application.yml for the three named circuit breakers
hints: Each circuit breaker is independent: seat-service opening doesn't affect event-db
Strictest for critical path (seat selection): lowest failureRateThreshold, shortest waitDuration
Lenient for non-critical (venue info): higher threshold, longer wait

exercises.title: Reading circuit breaker state programmatically
exercises.scenario: CircuitBreakerRegistry allows runtime inspection of circuit state.
exercises.instructions:
Write CircuitBreakerMonitor:
1. Inject CircuitBreakerRegistry
2. getState(name): returns the current state of a named circuit breaker
3. getMetrics(name): returns failure rate, slow call rate, number of calls
4. @Scheduled: log all circuit breaker states every 30 seconds
5. Show: how to check if circuit is OPEN before making a call
hints: CircuitBreakerRegistry: Spring bean auto-configured by resilience4j-spring-boot3
cb.getMetrics(): failure rate, slow call rate, buffered calls in sliding window
getAllCircuitBreakers(): iterate all named instances for dashboard/alerting

exercises.title: Test circuit breaker with a mocked failing client
exercises.scenario: Verify circuit breaker behavior: fallback fires after failures, circuit reopens.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @SpringBootTest test:
1. Mock the downstream client to always throw RuntimeException
2. Call the protected method 6 times (window=10, min=5, threshold=50%)
3. Verify: first 5 calls trigger fallback (real exceptions count)
4. Verify: circuit is now OPEN (6th failure crossed 50%)
5. Verify: fallback return value is correct
hints: cb.reset(): programmatic circuit reset to CLOSED state for clean test
@MockBean: replaces the real client with a Mockito mock in Spring context
5 failures with failureRateThreshold=30 and minimumNumberOfCalls=3: 5/5=100% ≥ 30% → OPEN

exercises.title: Slow call detection circuit breaker
exercises.scenario: Circuit opens even on successful but slow calls — prevents thread exhaustion.
exercises.instructions:
Configure and test slow call circuit breaker:
1. application.yml: slowCallDurationThreshold=2s, slowCallRateThreshold=60%
2. ZerodhaMarketDataService.getMarketDepth(symbol): wrapped with CB
3. Fallback: return empty market depth with 'delayed' flag
4. Test: mock client to sleep 3 seconds (slow but successful)
5. After threshold met: verify circuit opens despite no failures
hints: slowCallDurationThreshold: calls longer than this are marked 'slow' even if successful
slowCallRateThreshold: independent threshold from failureRateThreshold
Either threshold can independently open the circuit

exercises.title: Circuit breaker event listener for alerting
exercises.scenario: Subscribe to circuit breaker state change events for monitoring.
exercises.instructions:
Write CircuitBreakerEventListener:
1. Subscribe to state transition events (CLOSED→OPEN, OPEN→CLOSED etc)
2. On OPEN: log CRITICAL + simulate PagerDuty alert
3. On CLOSED (recovery): log INFO + simulate recovery notification
4. Show how to register the listener via CircuitBreakerRegistry
5. @SpringBootTest: trigger failure, verify OPEN event fires
hints: getEventPublisher().onStateTransition(): subscribe to state change events
StateTransition enum: CLOSED_TO_OPEN, OPEN_TO_HALF_OPEN, HALF_OPEN_TO_CLOSED etc
@PostConstruct: register after all circuit breakers are initialized

exercises.title: What happens with a void fallback for a non-void method?
exercises.scenario: Fallback signature must exactly match the original method's return type.
hints: Resilience4j does runtime method discovery, not compile-time checking
Return type must match exactly — void for void methods, T for T-returning methods
Multiple fallbacks: Resilience4j selects by most specific exception type

exercises.title: Razorpay payment service with circuit breaker protection
exercises.scenario: Full circuit breaker for external payment gateway calls.
exercises.instructions:
Build Razorpay's PaymentGatewayService:
1. chargeCard(request): calls external Visa/Mastercard gateway
2. Circuit breaker 'payment-gateway': failureRate=20%, window=20, min=10, wait=60s
3. Fallback: queue for async retry, return PENDING status
4. refundPayment(paymentId): separate CB 'refund-gateway'
5. Test (2 tests): charge returns PENDING on fallback, retry queue receives it
hints: ConcurrentLinkedQueue: thread-safe queue for retry items
PENDING status: tells the caller the charge will be retried
@BeforeEach reset(): circuit must be CLOSED before each test

exercises.title: Actuator health check integration for circuit breakers
exercises.scenario: Expose circuit breaker state in /actuator/health for operational monitoring.
exercises.instructions:
Configure and verify Actuator circuit breaker health:
1. application.yml: expose health + circuitbreakers actuator endpoints
2. Show health JSON structure for CLOSED and OPEN states
3. Write a custom HealthIndicator that flags DEGRADED when any CB is OPEN
4. @SpringBootTest: trigger circuit open, verify health shows DOWN/DEGRADED
5. Explain: why exposing CB state in health matters for Kubernetes/load balancers
hints: HealthIndicator: Spring Actuator interface for /actuator/health contribution
Health.down(): status DOWN with details map
Kubernetes readiness: removes unhealthy pods from load balancer rotation

exercises.title: Swiggy order service — multi-CB cascade protection
exercises.scenario: Order placement calls 4 services — each protected by an independent circuit breaker.
exercises.instructions:
Build Swiggy order flow with 4 circuit breakers:
1. validateAddress(orderId) → 'address-validator' CB, fallback: skip validation
2. checkRestaurantAvailability(restaurantId) → 'restaurant-api' CB, fallback: assume open
3. applyDiscounts(order) → 'discount-engine' CB, fallback: no discount
4. notifyRestaurant(orderId) → 'notification' CB, fallback: queue for later
5. placeOrder() calls all 4 in sequence
6. Test (2 tests): all services fail → order still completes with degraded fallbacks
hints: Each CB is independent: 4 breakers can each be in different states
Core order (PLACED) never blocked: all non-critical calls have safe fallbacks
@BeforeEach reset all: 4 separate circuit breakers to reset

exercises.title: TIME_BASED sliding window circuit breaker
exercises.scenario: TIME_BASED window tracks calls in the last N seconds instead of last N calls.
exercises.instructions:
Configure and use TIME_BASED sliding window:
1. application.yml: slidingWindowType=TIME_BASED, slidingWindowSize=30 (30 seconds)
2. Explain: when TIME_BASED is better than COUNT_BASED
3. Write application.yml for both types with comparison
4. FlipkartInventoryService with TIME_BASED CB
5. Explain: what happens during idle periods with each window type
hints: TIME_BASED: slidingWindowSize = number of SECONDS to look back
COUNT_BASED: slidingWindowSize = number of CALLS to track
Idle period: COUNT_BASED holds old data; TIME_BASED auto-expires old failures

exercises.title: Complete BookMyShow resilience — circuit breaker synthesis
exercises.scenario: Full microservice protection for a ticket booking flow.
exercises.instructions:
Build BookMyShow's complete resilient booking service:
1. 4 circuit breakers: event-api(50%), seat-service(30%), payment-gw(20%), notification(80%)
2. BookingService.book(request): calls all 4, graceful degradation on each failure
3. Payment failure → return PAYMENT_FAILED (can't degrade this one)
4. EventListener for CB state changes → logs ALERT on OPEN
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): all pass with mocked clients

All tests pass.
hints: Payment is critical: fallback returns FAILED (not a silent degradation)
@BeforeEach: try-catch reset for CBs that may not exist yet in test context
@PostConstruct listener: registered after all CBs initialized by Spring
